# Implementation Plan: Minimal PoC

**Branch**: `001-minimal-poc` | **Date**: 2026-04-12 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)  
**Input**: Feature specification from `/Users/akollegger/Developer/akollegger/aie-matrix/specs/001-minimal-poc/spec.md`

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit-plan`. Related proposal: [RFC-0001](../../proposals/rfc/0001-minimal-poc.md). Wire protocol: [ADR-0001](../../proposals/adr/0001-mcp-ghost-wire-protocol.md).

## Summary

Deliver a runnable local proof-of-concept: world backends (Colyseus authoritative state + MCP `world-api` for ghost navigation + REST `registry` + dev `auth`), Phaser spectator, flat `ghosts/` packages (`ts-client/`, `python-client/` stub, `random-house/` reference GhostHouse with embedded random walker, `tck/`), and repository map assets. A contributor clones the repo, starts the combined server, opens the browser, starts `ghosts/random-house/` for adoption-driven movement, and runs `ghosts/tck/` for compatibility validation. Pre-implementation choices called out in the RFC (transport, hex orientation, schema sync, adoption UX) are resolved for planning in [research.md](./research.md); interface shapes are captured under [contracts/](./contracts/).

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript on Node.js (active LTS) for `server/*`, `client/phaser`, `shared/types`, and `ghosts/*` packages; Python 3.11+ for `ghosts/python-client/` stub only.  
**Primary Dependencies**: Colyseus (authoritative room + WebSocket broadcast to spectators); Phaser 3 (spectator); MCP server/client libraries for `world-api` and SDKs; minimal HTTP stack for `registry` and static or dev-server delivery of Phaser build; JWT handling in `auth` (dev secret for PoC).  
**Storage**: N/A for PoC — in-memory Colyseus room state and ephemeral registry/adoption data unless a later task introduces a tiny on-disk fixture.  
**Testing**: `ghosts/tck/` as primary automated compatibility driver against a live local stack; package-level smoke scripts (documented in `quickstart.md` and package READMEs) for server boot and browser sanity.  
**Target Platform**: Local developer machines (macOS, Linux; Windows best-effort if dependencies allow).  
**Project Type**: Multi-package monorepo (`server/`, `client/`, `shared/`, `ghosts/`, `maps/`) introduced by this feature per RFC layout.  
**Package manager**: [pnpm](https://pnpm.io/) workspaces at the repository root (`pnpm-workspace.yaml`, shared `pnpm-lock.yaml`); TypeScript packages use the `@aie-matrix/*` scope. `ghosts/python-client/` remains Python-only (`pyproject.toml`) outside the pnpm workspace.  
**Performance Goals**: Not a PoC gate; spectator updates should meet spec SC-003 (order-of-a-second visibility) on a laptop.  
**Constraints**: Ghosts never connect to Colyseus directly; movement validation only in `world-api`; GhostHouse runtime outside world packages; flat `ghosts/` per FR-019; documented PoC-only shortcuts (single server process, in-process `world-api` → Colyseus).  
**Scale/Scope**: Small hex map asset; two concurrent ghosts in local demo; minimal TCK step sequence (full TCK spec deferred per RFC).

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

- **Proposal linkage**: `proposals/rfc/0001-minimal-poc.md` and `proposals/adr/0001-mcp-ghost-wire-protocol.md` bound this plan; scope matches the feature spec boundary and out-of-scope list.
- **Boundaries**: World vs ghost-side vs spectator paths match `docs/architecture.md` intent and RFC; PoC shortcut — `world-api` invokes Colyseus in-process — is explicit in [research.md](./research.md) and not presented as production topology.
- **Contracts**: `specs/001-minimal-poc/contracts/` holds registry, MCP ghost surface, spectator state, map, TCK, and local-setup contracts aligned to IC-001–IC-007.
- **Verification**: Each user story in the spec maps to a verification path in [quickstart.md](./quickstart.md); runnable code additions require documented smoke steps per constitution III.
- **Documentation impact**: `README.md`, `docs/architecture.md` (PoC shortcuts and layout), `docs/project-overview.md` (only if demo language changes contributor-facing story), `CONTRIBUTING.md` (commands/sign-off if new workflow), package READMEs under each new runnable package, optional `ghosts/README.md` per spec.

### Constitution Check (post–Phase 1)

- Design artifacts (`research.md`, `data-model.md`, `contracts/*`, `quickstart.md`) complete; no unresolved `NEEDS CLARIFICATION` items remain in [research.md](./research.md).
- Contract set covers cross-package and cross-language touchpoints required before implementation sign-off.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-minimal-poc/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Phase 0
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1
├── contracts/           # Phase 1
└── tasks.md             # From /speckit-tasks (not created by plan)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
/Users/akollegger/Developer/akollegger/aie-matrix/
├── server/
│   ├── colyseus/
│   ├── world-api/       # MCP server; movement rules; Colyseus updates (PoC: in-process)
│   ├── registry/        # REST: GhostHouse registration, adoption
│   └── auth/
├── client/
│   └── phaser/
├── shared/
│   └── types/           # Canonical TS types + MCP tool schemas
├── ghosts/              # Flat namespace (FR-019); no nested subtype dirs
│   ├── ts-client/
│   ├── python-client/
│   ├── random-house/    # Reference GhostHouse + embedded random walker
│   └── tck/
├── maps/                # Tiled sample hex map + assets
└── specs/001-minimal-poc/  # This feature’s plan, research, contracts
```

**Structure Decision**: Adopt the RFC-0001 layout as-is. Top-level `server/`, `client/`, `shared/`, `ghosts/`, and `maps/` are justified by the RFC/ADR pair and feature spec; `ghosts/` stays a single flat list of packages (`*-client/`, `*-house/`, `tck/`) per FR-019. No additional top-level directories for the PoC.

## Complexity Tracking

> No constitution violations requiring justification; PoC shortcuts are documented in `research.md` and scoped to local development.
